hypothesis: Systematic fine-tuning of an LLM on a curated dataset of physics-based simulator trajectories—where each trajectory encodes the sequence of actions, intermediate states, and thermodynamic outcomes for a given binary oxide system—will produce a model whose internal representations explicitly encode activation energy barriers and equilibrium phase boundaries, enabling zero-shot generalization to ternary or higher-order oxide systems with success rates significantly exceeding those of the base GPT-4o model without such fine-tuning, as measured by phase fraction accuracy in the same JMA-CALPHAD simulation framework.
